title: Macros 1
description: Macros are defined with `macro_rules!` and allow you to write code that writes code (metaprogramming).
instructions: Fix the macro definition so the code compiles.
hints: This macro definition looks correct - it matches empty parentheses and prints a message.
Macros must be defined before they are used (or use #[macro_export]).
Check that the macro name and invocation match.
explanation: Macros in Rust are defined with `macro_rules!`. The pattern `() => { ... }` matches an empty invocation `my_macro!()`. Macros operate on the token stream at compile time, generating code before it is compiled. They are invoked with `!` to distinguish them from function calls. Note the semicolon after the closing brace of the arm.
